guest_credentials: Former professional cyclist and current team director at Alpecin and Modern Adventure Pro Cycling
key_claims: Modern Adventure Pro Cycling has 30+ staff members just for wheel support and logistics at Roubaix
Equipment evolution has shifted from 21mm tires at 140 PSI to 32-36mm tires at 55-65 PSI, making cobbles more ridable
Tire inserts help the tires stay rideable longer until a wheel change can be made
Larger WorldTour teams like UAE, Alpecin, and Ineos have 50-80 staff at Roubaix compared to Modern Adventure's 30+
Modern Adventure is using Monza Factor frames with SRAM Force 1x groupset and Michelin tubeless tires with inserts for Roubaix
specific_numbers: 30 people outside staff for wheels and feeding
15 bikes built for this one-day race
50-80 staff for major teams like UAE and Alpecin at Roubaix
cluster_reasoning: This is a pro story and cultural deep-dive featuring George Hincapie discussing monument racing tactics, team logistics, and the evolution of bike technology at Roubaix—classic community content about cycling culture and professional cycling history, with secondary coaching relevance for race tactics.
